summary: Full backend + frontend testing of QSVM Brain Tumor Detection dashboard. Backend pytest: 7/7 passed (root, metrics shape, predict happy path with ~5s simulated latency, predict 400 on non-image, predict 400 on empty, history shape, delete + 404). Frontend Playwright: navigation (dashboard/insights/history), drag-drop file upload, preview, analyze-button disabled-state, processing overlay with all 4 sub-elements (elapsed/estimated/progress-bar/stages), results panel (diagnosis/inference-time/confidence-gauge/probability-chart), high-confidence-badge vs low-confidence-warning toggle, insights page (confusion matrix cells, accuracy text), history page (5 filter buttons, delete button + toast 'Scan removed from history', empty state after delete) — all verified working end-to-end against the live preview URL.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('shutdown'); prefer lifespan handlers.
POST /api/predict performs asyncio.sleep up to ~10s while holding the request — acceptable as a mock, but in real deployment would block under load; consider a background-task + polling pattern.
DELETE /api/history/{id} returns 200 with {'deleted': 0} when id is unknown instead of 404 — minor; client treats it as success either way.
thumbnail_url stored as full-size base64 (no resizing) — fine for small MRIs but could bloat Mongo for large uploads.
seed_data_creation: One scan was created during predict test then deleted via history delete test — DB left clean.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend simulated inference sleeps ~5–10s — use timeout>=30s for /api/predict. Insights `sep-{class}` testid uses kebab-case e.g. sep-no-tumor / sep-glioma. History delete on unknown id returns 200 deleted=0 (not 404).
